name: shape-calendar
title: Shape Calendar
description: Plan your active life with Shape Calendar — search, create, and manage your training activities
author: samuelkraft

commands.name: search-workouts
commands.title: Search Activities
commands.description: Search and browse your planned and completed activities

commands.name: today
commands.title: Today's Workout
commands.description: Shows today's planned or completed workout

commands.name: this-week
commands.title: This Week
commands.description: See your training plan for the current week

tools.name: get-activities
tools.title: Get Activities
tools.description: Get activities from Shape Calendar for the current user, optionally filtered by date range, sport type, or completion status

tools.name: get-latest-activity
tools.title: Get Latest Activity
tools.description: Get the most recent completed activity from Shape Calendar

tools.name: get-upcoming-activities
tools.title: Get Upcoming Activities
tools.description: Get planned (not yet completed) activities for the next two weeks from Shape Calendar

tools.name: create-activity
tools.title: Create Activity
tools.description: Create a new training activity in Shape Calendar. Requires date, title, and sport type. Distance is in meters, duration in seconds.

tools.name: delete-activity
tools.title: Delete Activity
tools.description: Delete an activity from Shape Calendar by its ID

tools.name: batch-create-activities
tools.title: Batch Create Activities
tools.description: Create multiple training activities at once in Shape Calendar (up to 100). Each requires date, title, and sport type. Distance is in meters, duration in seconds.

tools.name: batch-delete-activities
tools.title: Batch Delete Activities
tools.description: Delete multiple activities at once from Shape Calendar by their IDs (up to 100)

tools.name: batch-update-activities
tools.title: Batch Update Activities
tools.description: Update multiple activities at once in Shape Calendar (up to 100). Each must include an id plus any fields to change.

ai.instructions: Help with Shape Calendar training tasks. Use get-activities for questions about past workouts or training over a specific period. Use get-latest-activity when asked about the most recent workout. Use get-upcoming-activities for questions about what's planned or coming up. Use create-activity to schedule a single workout. Use batch-create-activities when creating multiple workouts at once (e.g. a training plan or week of workouts). Use batch-update-activities to modify multiple activities at once (e.g. mark all as completed, reschedule a week). Use delete-activity to remove a single activity — always confirm with the user first and use get-activities to find the ID. Use batch-delete-activities to remove multiple activities at once. Distance is always in meters, duration in seconds. Sport types are: run, bike, swim, hike, yoga, nordicski, strength, other. For structured interval workouts (e.g. 5x400m repeats), set target to 'steps' and include a steps array with warmup, interval, recovery, repeat, and cooldown steps. Only use steps for workouts with specific interval structure — not for simple runs or strength training. Keep titles short without distance/duration.

evals.input: @shape-calendar what did I do this week?
@shape-calendar what do I have planned for next week?
@shape-calendar how much have I biked this month?
@shape-calendar plan a yoga session for Friday
@shape-calendar delete my morning run from yesterday
@shape-calendar create a training plan for next week with a run on Monday, bike on Wednesday, and swim on Friday
@shape-calendar clear all my planned activities for this week

preferences.name: apiToken
preferences.title: API Token
preferences.description: Your Shape Calendar API token (starts with shape_)

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
